comments.author: @Emargonaut
comments.text: Congratulations to Bodø/Glimt & hats off to the  supporters that traveled all the way from the Arctic Circle to carry their team to the playoffs. As an Atlético de Madrid fan, I have to acknowledge how well Bodø/Glimt played, exploiting our team's deficiencies and getting an unexpected win. Despite how dissapointed I am, I am also happy that you in particular were the ones responsible for the upset. I've been following Bodø since last year's UEL semifinals, and yesterday you showed once again great courage and believed in your players, and you did what almost no club can do: defeat Atlético at its bastion 👏👏👏. I'm rooting for you guys. Best of luck for the next stages of the UCL 🍀.

comments.text:
Nesta about R9:

"I played against both of them. When I played against Cristiano and Messi, I was already 37 and no longer at my peak.
But when I faced Brazilian Ronaldo, I was 22 years old, and it was the UEFA Cup Final. I had never seen a player like him. I sat in the room after the match, asking myself WHAT MORE I COULD HAVE DONE.That night, I COULD NOT DO MORE! Because I realized that "I FOUND THE STRONGEST"
My abilities were not enough to stop him. I’m not saying Messi and Cristiano are bad players — but you can play against them.However, against R9.......IMPOSSIBLE!"

Presentación e Impacto Inmediato
El jugador ya pasó el reconocimiento médico en la Clínica Universidad de Navarra y fue presentado el pasado martes en el Riyadh Air Metropolitano.

Bajo las órdenes del Cholo: Simeone buscaba un perfil polivalente que pudiera actuar tanto de segundo punta como de extremo, aportando ese "uno contra uno" y la capacidad goleadora que Lookman demostró en Italia (donde dejó un registro de 55 goles en 137 partidos con la Atalanta).

Debut inminente: Se espera que pueda entrar en la convocatoria para el trascendental partido de cuartos de final de la Copa del Rey contra el Real Betis este mismo jueves.

comments.author: @Kodfizh19
comments.text: Coach, we got exactly what we deserved today. Szoboszlai scored such a stunning goal, and we started the second half so strongly—anyone could see we were dominating. But as soon as we took the lead, we reverted to that same old mistake: slowing the game down and trying to sit back. Why change what was working? Why stop the fast-paced attacking football that could have easily gotten us a second goal? We had them on the ropes, but instead, we invited pressure. It’s the same painful lesson we’ve failed to learn over and over again. We only started rushing again after they took the lead with less than 10 minutes left. It’s always like this!
